subject: Anthropology
title: Post-Growth Economies
description: A micro-lesson that makes Post-Growth Economies usable.

hook.text: What if the goal isn't more growth? Indigenous and degrowth movements ask: can an economy thrive without expanding?

buildup.text: Post-growth thinking challenges the assumption that economic growth equals progress and well-being.

discovery.text: Many Indigenous economies prioritize balance, reciprocity, and sustainability over accumulation and expansion.

twist.text: Degrowth isn't poverty. It's restructuring economies around well-being, ecology, and sufficiency instead of GDP.

climax.text: Post-growth asks the ultimate economic question: when is enough? The answer may determine the planet's future.

punchline.text: The richest economy might be the one that knows when to stop.

quiz.question: What do post-growth economies prioritize?
options: Well-being, ecology, and sufficiency over endless expansion
